loginPrompt.title: Login Required
loginPrompt.message: Please log in or sign up to continue. Your learning progress will be saved automatically.

dashboard.title: Learning Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Hi {{name}}, here is the full picture of your learning data.
dashboard.subtitleGuest: Please log in to view your learning progress.
dashboard.learnedToday: You have studied {{minutes}} minutes today, goal {{goal}} minutes. Keep it up!
dashboard.moduleTip: Consistent practice will gradually raise these metrics
dashboard.heatmapSubtitle: Each cell is one day · darker means more study time

courses.seoTitle: Language Courses — A1 to C1 | LangOria
courses.seoDescription: Browse structured language courses from A1 to C2 across English, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Thai, and Cantonese. Spaced-repetition vocab, listening drills, and speaking practice.
courses.title: Leveled Course System
courses.subtitle: Filter by language and level — from zero to mastery.
courses.noResults: No courses match. Try a different filter.
courses.disclaimer: Level labels (CEFR / JLPT / HSK / TOPIK) are for indicative alignment only, to facilitate cross-system comparison. CEFR is a trademark of the Council of Europe, JLPT is a trademark of the Japan Foundation, HSK is a trademark of Hanban, and TOPIK is a trademark of the National Institute for International Education of Korea. This project is not certified or authorized by any of these official organizations; level achievement data is an internal estimate.

entryCards.placement: Not sure where to start? Try the placement test
entryCards.placementDesc: 6 adaptive questions to pinpoint your starting level · no login required
entryCards.alphabet: Start from letters: Asian language basics
entryCards.alphabetDesc: Hiragana / Katakana / Hangul / Pinyin / Jyutping / Thai · tap a character to hear it
entryCards.reading: Reading comprehension · complete your CEFR skills
entryCards.readingDesc: Graded reading + vocab notes + comprehension questions · auto-records your best score
entryCards.league: Weekly leaderboard · compete for Master tier
entryCards.leagueDesc: XP accumulates weekly · Bronze → Silver → Gold → Platinum → Diamond → Master · promotions and demotions settled every Monday
entryCards.cefr: CEFR self-assessment · what can I do?
entryCards.cefrDesc: Self-assess A1-C2 by Can-Do statements · complements the placement test · auto-infers your level
entryCards.writingDesc: Essays / emails / summaries / stories / dialogues · three-dimensional scoring on word count + lexical diversity + keywords

en-a1-1.title: Zero to Beginner: Everyday English
en-a1-1.description: Greetings, numbers, and daily expressions. Start speaking from day one.

en-a2-1.title: Daily Life English
en-a2-1.description: Shopping, dining, and routines. Build practical A2 vocabulary.

en-b1-1.title: Express Yourself in English
en-b1-1.description: Talk about opinions, plans, and experiences with confidence.

en-b2-1.title: English for Work & Study
en-b2-1.description: Meetings, emails, presentations, and academic discussions.

en-c1-1.title: Advanced English: Culture & Ideas
en-c1-1.description: Debate complex topics and understand nuanced expressions.

ja-n5-1.title: Hiragana & Katakana Basics
ja-n5-1.description: Learn the two kana scripts and simple greetings.

ja-n3-1.title: Intermediate Japanese
ja-n3-1.description: Read short articles and follow natural dialogues.

ja-n2-1.title: Business & Study Abroad Japanese
ja-n2-1.description: Professional communication and JLPT N2 strategies.

ja-n1-1.title: Advanced Japanese Mastery
ja-n1-1.description: Native-level reading, keigo, and professional fluency.

ko-beginner-1.title: Hangeul & Basic Korean
ko-beginner-1.description: Master the Korean alphabet and foundational phrases.

ko-intermediate-1.title: Korean via K-Drama & K-Pop
ko-intermediate-1.description: Learn natural expressions through dramas and songs.

ko-advanced-1.title: Advanced Korean: TOPIK & Business
ko-advanced-1.description: High-level grammar for tests and the workplace.

zh-hsk1-1.title: Pinyin & HSK 1 Chinese
zh-hsk1-1.description: Start with tones, pinyin, and basic characters.

zh-hsk3-1.title: HSK 3 Social Chinese
zh-hsk3-1.description: Hold everyday conversations and read simple texts.

zh-hsk5-1.title: HSK 5 News & Academic Chinese
zh-hsk5-1.description: Understand news, essays, and formal speech.

es-a1-1.title: Zero to Beginner: Spanish
es-a1-1.description: Greetings, travel phrases, and pronunciation basics.

es-b1-1.title: Express Yourself in Spanish
es-b1-1.description: Share opinions, stories, and travel plans with ease.

es-c1-1.title: Advanced Spanish: Literature & Academia
es-c1-1.description: Analyze texts and discuss culture in depth.

fr-b2-1.title: French Expression & Writing
fr-b2-1.description: Write clear texts and argue your point in French.

fr-c1-1.title: Advanced French: Culture & Philosophy
fr-c1-1.description: Explore ideas, arts, and nuanced expression.

de-c1-1.title: Advanced German: Literature & Ideas
de-c1-1.description: Read literature and discuss complex topics.

it-b1-1.title: Converse in Italian
it-b1-1.description: Talk about travel, food, culture, and daily life.

it-c1-1.title: Advanced Italian: Art & Literature
it-c1-1.description: Dive into Italian arts, literature, and rhetoric.

th-beg-1.title: Thai Tones & Basics
th-beg-1.description: Master tones, consonants, and first conversations.

th-int-1.title: Travel & Conversation Thai
th-int-1.description: Get around, order food, and chat with locals.

th-adv-1.title: Advanced Thai: Culture & Fluency
th-adv-1.description: Understand media, culture, and idiomatic Thai.

yue-beg-1.title: Cantonese Tones & Basics
yue-beg-1.description: Learn Jyutping, tones, and everyday phrases.

yue-int-1.title: Daily Cantonese Conversations
yue-int-1.description: Talk about life, food, and getting around.

yue-adv-1.title: Advanced Cantonese: Film & Culture
yue-adv-1.description: Understand movies, slang, and local culture.

levelMeta.disclaimer: Level labels (CEFR / JLPT / HSK / TOPIK) are for indicative alignment only, to facilitate cross-system comparison. CEFR is a trademark of the Council of Europe, JLPT is a trademark of the Japan Foundation, HSK is a trademark of Hanban, and TOPIK is a trademark of the National Institute for International Education of Korea. This project is not certified or authorized by any of these official organizations; level achievement data is an internal estimate.

A1.name: Beginner
goals: Master 50 core sentence patterns for everyday greetings and self-introductions
Recognize about 700 high-frequency words
Understand slow everyday conversations (around 60% comprehension)
Write simple texts of 50–100 words

A2.name: Elementary
goals: Handle common everyday situations such as shopping, asking directions, and ordering food
Recognize about 1,500 words
Read short notices, advertisements, and emails
Write 100–150 word descriptions of daily life

B1.name: Intermediate
goals: Deal with most situations while traveling
Recognize about 2,500–3,000 words
Read everyday written materials and short news articles
Give simple, connected spoken or written accounts of experiences and opinions

B2.name: Upper-Intermediate
goals: Understand the main ideas of complex texts and abstract topics
Recognize about 4,000–5,000 words
Interact with native speakers with reasonable fluency and spontaneity
Write detailed opinion and argument essays

C1.name: Advanced
goals: Understand long, demanding texts and implicit meaning
Recognize about 8,000 words
Express ideas fluently and spontaneously for social, academic, and professional use
Write clear, well-structured complex texts

C2.name: Mastery
goals: Understand almost everything heard or read with little effort
Recognize about 16,000 words
Express subtle nuances with near-native precision
Handle complex texts in academic, literary, and professional fields

A1.name: Beginner
goals: Master the basics of Cantonese's nine tones and six contours
Recognize about 800 common Cantonese words
Handle everyday greetings, self-introductions, and ordering food
Understand slow Cantonese conversations

A2.name: Elementary
goals: Recognize about 1,500 Cantonese words
Handle shopping, asking directions, and casual chatting
Understand everyday conversations at moderate speed

B1.name: Intermediate
goals: Recognize about 3,000 Cantonese words
Talk about a wide range of familiar topics
Catch the main information in Hong Kong dramas and Cantonese news

B2.name: Upper-Intermediate
goals: Recognize about 5,000 Cantonese words
Speak fluently with native speakers
Understand fast-paced Hong Kong movies and news

N5.name: Beginner
goals: Master hiragana, katakana, and about 100 basic kanji
Recognize about 800 words
Understand slow everyday conversations
Read basic short passages and notices

N4.name: Late Beginner
goals: Recognize about 1,500 words and around 300 kanji
Read basic texts on familiar everyday topics
Understand everyday conversations at moderate speed

N3.name: Intermediate
goals: Recognize about 3,500–4,000 words and around 600 kanji
Read summaries of everyday written materials
Understand everyday conversations near natural speed

N2.name: Upper-Intermediate
goals: Recognize about 6,000 words and around 1,000 kanji
Read newspaper commentaries and general essays
Understand coherent conversations and news at natural speed

N1.name: Advanced
goals: Recognize about 10,000–15,000 words and around 2,000 kanji
Read logically complex and abstract texts
Understand lectures, news, and discussions at natural speed

TOPIK1.name: Beginner
goals: Master reading and writing Hangeul
Recognize about 800 basic words
Introduce yourself, greet, order food, and ask directions
Read simple everyday short texts

TOPIK2.name: Elementary
goals: Recognize about 1,500–2,000 words
Handle daily tasks and use public facilities
Read simple short texts and write short notes

TOPIK3.name: Intermediate
goals: Recognize about 3,000–4,000 words
Handle daily life independently and understand social relationships
Follow familiar news topics

TOPIK4.name: Upper-Intermediate
goals: Recognize about 4,000–6,000 words
Use public facilities and maintain social relationships
Read newspapers and news (study-abroad threshold)

TOPIK5.name: Advanced
goals: Recognize about 6,000–8,000 words
Communicate professionally and understand unfamiliar topics
Use formal and informal language appropriately

TOPIK6.name: Mastery
goals: Recognize about 8,000–10,000+ words
Approach native-like fluency
Express opinions precisely and handle academic/professional situations

HSK1.name: Beginner
goals: Master pinyin, tones, and about 500 common characters (HSK 3.0)
Understand and use basic greetings and simple phrases
Understand slow everyday conversations

HSK2.name: Basic
goals: Recognize about 1,200 words (HSK 3.0)
Communicate simply about familiar everyday topics
Read short texts

HSK3.name: Elementary
goals: Recognize about 2,200 words (HSK 3.0)
Handle basic communication about life, study, and work
Understand everyday conversations at normal speed

HSK4.name: Intermediate
goals: Recognize about 3,245 words (HSK 3.0)
Communicate fairly fluently on a broad range of topics
Read newspaper articles and understand films/TV shows

HSK5.name: Advanced
goals: Recognize about 4,316 words (HSK 3.0)
Read Chinese newspapers, watch films/TV, and write short essays
Understand radio and news broadcasts

HSK6.name: Advanced Plus
goals: Recognize about 5,456 words (HSK 3.0)
Comfortably understand content across listening, speaking, reading, and writing
Write longer argumentative and academic essays

HSK7.name: Advanced Extension
goals: Recognize 6,000+ words (HSK 3.0 Advanced)
Read long academic, professional, and literary texts
Understand lectures, debates, and radio programs
Write well-structured arguments on complex topics
grammar: Breaking down and reconstructing long sentences

HSK8.name: Proficiency
goals: Recognize 8,000+ words (HSK 3.0 Proficiency)
Understand professional Chinese documents without barriers
Participate in high-level academic debates and impromptu speeches
Write academic and literary works close to native-speaker level
grammar: Flexible switching between written and spoken registers

HSK9.name: Mastery
goals: Recognize 11,000+ words (HSK 3.0 Mastery)
Handle any Chinese-language situation freely, approaching native-speaker level
Critically analyze complex texts and grasp implicit meaning
Perform proficiently in academic, diplomatic, and literary-creation fields
grammar: Fusion of classical and modern Chinese

learn.wordMemoryDesc: Flip flashcards, active recall, strengthen long-term memory
learn.speakingDesc: Listen and repeat, build muscle memory

grammarModule.question: Question {{current}} / {{total}}
grammarModule.tip1: Read the whole sentence first, then judge the grammar structure.
grammarModule.tip2: If unsure, go with your intuition first.
grammarModule.tip3: Every explanation is worth reading carefully.

speakingModule.title: Repeat This Sentence
speakingModule.startHint: Tap to start recording and read aloud
speakingModule.privacyHint: Tip: The browser will not upload audio; recording is for local timing only.
speakingModule.loginToStart: Log in to start speaking practice

listeningModule.title: Listen and Fill
listeningModule.playHint: Tap play (browser will read this text aloud)
listeningModule.tip1: Listen blindly once and see how much you understand.
listeningModule.tip2: Then fill in the blanks while reading to identify weak forms and linking.
listeningModule.tip3: Play repeatedly and read along at the same time.
listeningModule.tip4: After checking, read the script aloud to reinforce the sounds.

review.noCardsForLang: No cards due for this language.
review.addedAutomatically: Cards are added automatically as you practice.
review.principle3: Easy cards are pushed further out.

conversation.unsupportedBrowser: Speech recognition is not supported in this browser.
conversation.didNotHear: Didn't catch that. Please try again.
conversation.fallbackBranch: No exact match — the AI will continue naturally.
conversation.sceneHint: Reply naturally in the target language.
conversation.tip3: Use voice input to practice speaking.
conversation.tip4: Don't worry about mistakes — just keep going.

home.seoTitle: Learn English, Japanese, Chinese, Korean & More | LangOria
home.seoDescription: Learn English, Japanese, Chinese, Korean, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Thai, and Cantonese with spaced-repetition vocabulary, listening drills, and speaking practice. Bite-sized 10-minute daily lessons.

hero.title: Today, let the world
hero.welcomeBack: Welcome back, {{name}}! You have studied {{minutes}} minutes today, goal {{goal}} minutes. Keep going!
hero.guest: Choose a language you want to conquer and build your learning habit starting today.

languages.choose: Choose the language you are learning
languages.standaloneDesc: Pick the language you are learning. Each course is leveled, with spaced-repetition vocab, native-speaker audio, and a daily 10-minute loop.

steps.title: A proven 3-step path from beginner to fluent
steps.desc: Each learner is different, but the path to fluency is always the same: input, output, and feedback. LangOria turns that loop into a 10-minute daily ritual.

items.title: Diagnose your level
items.desc: Pick your target language and starting level. We build a personalized path based on what you already know.

items.title: Daily 10-minute loop
items.desc: Vocab + grammar + listening + speaking, scheduled by spaced repetition so you remember what you learn.

items.title: Speak with confidence
items.desc: Shadow real sentences, get instant feedback, and track your streak to keep the habit alive.

features.title: Four Core Exercises for Complete Fluency
features.desc: Input · Output · Feedback — build real speaking confidence.

items.title: Flashcard Words
items.desc: Spaced repetition — once remembered, never forgotten

items.title: Grammar Practice
items.desc: Instant feedback + explanations, rules made clear

items.title: Speaking Repeat
items.desc: Read aloud to build muscle memory in your tongue

items.title: Listening Training
items.desc: Real context, fill-in-the-blank, hear more clearly

courses.title: Featured Courses
courses.desc: Leveled, progressive, adaptable to multiple learning goals

stories.title: Real progress, real people
stories.desc: Hear from learners who turned a 10-minute daily habit into a language they can actually use.

items.name: Yuki, 27
items.quote: I tried three apps before. LangOria is the only one where I actually open it every day. The streak system works.

items.name: Carlos, 34
items.quote: The grammar explanations are short and clear. I went from A2 to B1 in three months while commuting.

items.name: Mei, 22
items.quote: I love that the interface is in Chinese but the words are in Japanese. It feels like the app actually knows me.

faq.title: Common questions, clear answers
faq.desc: Everything you need to know before you start.

items.a: Yes. The core learning experience — vocab, grammar, listening, speaking, and the community — is free. Premium features will be added later for power users.

items.q: Do I need to install anything?
items.a: No. LangOria runs in any modern browser. Mobile apps will follow once the web version stabilizes.
English, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, Spanish, French, and German. New languages are added every month based on learner demand.

items.q: How long until I see progress?
items.a: Most learners feel a clear difference in 4-6 weeks when they stick to the 10-minute daily loop. The streak system and spaced repetition are designed to make that habit stick.

items.q: Can I use it on my phone?
items.a: Yes. The site is fully responsive and works great on mobile browsers. Add it to your home screen for an app-like experience.

blog.title: Tips, guides, and learning science
blog.desc: Bite-sized articles that make your study time more effective.

items.title: Why 10 minutes a day beats 2 hours on weekends
items.excerpt: Spaced repetition is the most researched technique in learning science. Here is why shorter, more frequent sessions outperform long cramming.

items.title: How to remember 1000 words without burning out
items.excerpt: A practical 8-week plan built on the 3-2-1 rule, with examples across English, Japanese, and Spanish.

items.title: Shadowing: the speaking exercise that actually works
items.excerpt: Why repeating native audio is the fastest way to fix pronunciation, and a 5-step routine you can start today.

cta.title: Start your 10-minute daily ritual
cta.desc: Free forever. No credit card. Cancel anytime you want — but you probably won't.

faq.seoDescription: Answers to common questions about LangOria's spaced-repetition language learning: how the 10-minute loop works, pricing, supported languages, and account help.
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about LangOria.
faq.intro: Can't find the answer you're looking for? Reach out via the community, and our team will get back to you.

items.q: How do I create an account?
items.a: Click the Sign up button in the top right corner, enter your email and a password, choose your target language and native language, and you're in.

items.q: Do I need a credit card?
items.a: No. The core learning experience is free. You only need a payment method when you choose to upgrade to Premium in the future.

items.q: Can I change my target language later?
items.a: Yes. Go to Settings → Target Language at any time. All your progress stays tied to the language you were practicing.

items.q: How long should I study each day?
items.a: We recommend 10 minutes a day. Spaced repetition works best with consistent, short sessions — not cramming.

items.q: Why are explanations in my native language?
items.a: Research shows learning is faster when explanations come from your strongest language. The actual words you study are always in the target language.

items.q: Will my streak reset if I skip a day?
items.a: Yes, the streak resets if you skip a full day. The grace window is 36 hours to cover time zone differences.
Your learning data is stored on Supabase (Postgres) in a region near you. We do not sell any user data.

items.q: How do I delete my account?
items.a: Go to Profile → Danger Zone, and click Delete Account. All your data is removed within 7 days.

blog.ctaDesc: Create a free account and start your 10-minute daily loop today.

posts.title: Why 10 minutes a day beats 2 hours on weekends
posts.excerpt: Spaced repetition is the most researched technique in learning science. Here is why shorter, more frequent sessions outperform long cramming.
content: If you have ever crammed for an exam and forgotten everything two weeks later, you already know the problem with long, infrequent study sessions. The fix is not studying harder. The fix is studying more often.

Learning science has been clear on this for decades. Hermann Ebbinghaus discovered the forgetting curve in 1885: we lose newly learned information at a predictable rate unless we actively recall it at the right moment. The 'right moment' is just before you are about to forget.

This is exactly what spaced repetition does. LangOria schedules each word and grammar point for review at the moment you are most likely to forget it. Hit 'remembered' and the next review gets pushed further out. Hit 'not familiar' and the next review comes sooner.

The result: in 10 minutes a day, you cover more ground than 2 hours on Sunday, because every minute is spent on something your brain actually needs to recall. After 30 days, most learners have reviewed 600+ items — more than a single weekend cramming session could ever cover.
So if you have been telling yourself you don't have time to learn a language, you do. Ten minutes. Every day. That is the entire trick.

posts.title: How to remember 1000 words without burning out
posts.excerpt: A practical 8-week plan built on the 3-2-1 rule, with examples across English, Japanese, and Spanish.
content: Most language learners aim for 1000 words as a first milestone. The bad news: trying to memorize them all at once is a recipe for burnout. The good news: you do not need to memorize them. You need to meet them.
We use a simple 3-2-1 rule. Three encounters a day, two contexts per encounter, and one forced recall. That is it.

Day 1: learn 3 new words. See them in your flashcard, see them in a sentence, see them in a grammar example. Day 2: 3 more new words, plus 6 from yesterday. Day 7: 21 new words this week, but 80+ reviews. By the time a word has been reviewed 5 times, retention is over 90%.

Across 8 weeks, this adds up to 168 words at a pace of 3 per day. To hit 1000, you scale to 15 new words a day by month 3, when reviews are mostly cached. The 10-minute daily loop in LangOria handles this for you automatically.
The key insight: vocabulary is not a memory contest. It is a frequency game. Show up every day, trust the system, and the words will stick.

posts.title: Shadowing: the speaking exercise that actually works
posts.excerpt: Why repeating native audio is the fastest way to fix pronunciation, and a 5-step routine you can start today.
content: If you can read a sentence but cannot say it, you are not alone. Most learners spend 90% of their time reading and listening, and 10% speaking. That ratio is backwards.

Shadowing is the practice of listening to a native speaker and repeating what they say in real time, mimicking rhythm, intonation, and stress. It is the technique professional interpreters use to maintain fluency, and it works for any language.

The 5-step routine: (1) Listen to a sentence at native speed. (2) Listen again with the transcript. (3) Repeat at native speed while recording. (4) Compare your recording to the original. (5) Repeat 3 times before moving on.
Do this for 5 minutes a day. Within a month, your accent will start to shift noticeably. Within three months, you will be able to produce sentences you could not read aloud before.
LangOria's Speaking module is built around this exact idea. Pick a sentence, listen, repeat, repeat, repeat. No judging, no feedback anxiety. Just your voice and the model.
